title: Proxmox LVM-thin storage oversubscription, thin-pool data %, and metadata % saturation
dataSources: PRIMARY (custom HEC, no first-party Proxmox TA on Splunkbase): `index=virtualization` or `index=proxmox` (and optional `index=os` for nix-captured `lvs` lines) with `sourcetype=proxmox:storage` from the datacenter PVE `GET /api2/json/storage` (cluster-wide; filter to `type=lvmthin` in the poller or in SPL) exposing `storage`, `type`, `content`, `nodes`, `total`, `used`, `avail`, `used_fraction`, `enabled`, `active`, `shared` plus a stable `cluster_name` in your envelope. `sourcetype=proxmox:storage:status` from per-node `GET /api2/json/nodes/{node}/storage/{storage}/status` for the node-local view where `used_fraction` is the canonical on-wire Data% for many builds (coalesced in SPL to `data_percent` alongside `lvs`). `sourcetype=proxmox:lvm:thinpool` from a node poller (SSH, fleet agent, or systemd timer) that runs `lvs --reportformat=json -o lv_name,lv_size,data_percent,metadata_percent,pool_lv,vg_name --select 'lv_attr=~"V|t.*"'` and emits one JSON line per pool per node per 60s with `vg_name`, `lv_name`, `data_percent`, `metadata_percent`, `lv_size_bytes` — the only path that always exposes `metadata_percent` with vendor-stable semantics. `sourcetype=proxmox:vm:disk` from `GET /api2/json/cluster/resources?type=vm` (poll all pages) flattening per-VM disk `storage` and `maxdisk` (bytes) to sum declared sizes per `storage` and compute allocated:provisioned against pool size. TERTIARY: Splunk_TA_nix (Splunkbase 833) optional monitors for `lvs` and `pvesm` / `df -h` text when the API poller is not yet deployed, tagged as `sourcetype=proxmox:cli:thinpool` in your `props.conf` (not the primary for metadata math).
app: No first-party Splunk app covers end-to-end Proxmox VE capacity for LVM-thin: this use case requires a custom Heavy Forwarder or secure Linux HEC poller for the PVE REST endpoints above, a separate node script for the `lvs` JSON, and a Splunk HTTP Event Collector token on the indexers or intermediate gateway. For emergency gap-fill, Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunk_TA_nix, Splunkbase 833) can file-monitor `lvs` or `pvesm` output that you write from cron, but that path does not remove the need for the structured JSON from `lvs` if you want reliable `metadata_percent` at scale. Version-control the two CSV files `proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv` and `proxmox_node_owner.csv` with the app that carries `transforms.conf` and the saved search, store `PVEAPIToken=…` in a vault, and pin Python to 3.10+ for TLS to PVE 8006.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.34 — Proxmox LVM-thin data%, metadata%, allocated:provisioned oversubscription. SOURCES: proxmox:storage, proxmox:storage:status, proxmox:lvm:thinpool, proxmox:vm:disk; LOOKUPS: proxmox_lvmthin_owner, proxmox_node_owner; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:storage" earliest=-30m@m ]
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:storage:status" earliest=-30m@m ]
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool" earliest=-30m@m ]
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:vm:disk" earliest=-1h@h ]
| eval is_vm=if(sourcetype="proxmox:vm:disk", 1, 0)
| eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
| eval node=toString(coalesce(node, pve_node, host, ""))
| eval vg_name=toString(coalesce(vg_name, vg, ""))
| eval lv_name=toString(coalesce(lv_name, thinpool_lv, pool_lv, lvm_tmeta, pool_lv_name, ""))
| eval pve_storage=toString(coalesce(storage, store, pve_storage, storageid, id, ""))
| where is_vm=0
| eval s_type=lower(toString(coalesce(type, st_type, store_type, "")))
| where (sourcetype!="proxmox:storage") OR (like(s_type, "lvm%thin%")) OR (s_type="lvmthin") OR (s_type="lvm_thin") OR (len(s_type)=0)
| eval ufx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_fraction, usedFraction, "")), 10)
| eval data_api=if((sourcetype="proxmox:storage:status") OR (sourcetype="proxmox:storage"), if((isnotnull(ufx)) AND (ufx>0) AND (ufx<=1.0), round(ufx*100, 4), if((isnotnull(ufx)) AND (ufx>1.0), round(ufx, 4), null())), null())
| eval data_lvs=if(sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool", tonumber(toString(coalesce(data_percent, dataPercent, data_pct, "")), 10), null())
| eval meta_lvs=if(sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool", tonumber(toString(coalesce(metadata_percent, metadataPercent, meta_percent, metadatapercent, "")), 10), null())
| eval data_percent=coalesce(data_lvs, data_api, round(ufx*100, 4))
| eval tot_b=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total, total_bytes, tot_bytes, size_bytes, lv_size_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval pool_size_bytes=if(sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool", tonumber(toString(coalesce(lv_size_bytes, lvSizeBytes, tot_b, "0")), 10), tot_b)
| eval ro_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(pool_state, poolState, status, st, "")))
| eval pool_state=if((like(ro_raw, "%ro%")) OR (like(ro_raw, "%read%only%")) OR (ro_raw="r/o") OR (ro_raw="ro"), "ro", if(len(ro_raw)>0, ro_raw, null()))
| sort 0 + cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name, _time
| streamstats window=288 current=t first(data_percent) AS d_24h_ago first(pool_size_bytes) AS ps_24h_ago BY cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name
| eval g24=if((isnotnull(d_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(data_percent)), data_percent - d_24h_ago, null())
| eval grow_pct_per_h=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24>0), g24/24, null())
| eval eta_to_full_hours=if((isnotnull(grow_pct_per_h)) AND (grow_pct_per_h>0) AND (isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent<100), round((100 - data_percent) / grow_pct_per_h, 2), null())
| eval eta_to_full_hours=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24<=0), null(), eta_to_full_hours)
| eval eta_to_full_hours=if((isnotnull(eta_to_full_hours)) AND (eta_to_full_hours<0), null(), eta_to_full_hours)
| stats max(_time) AS _time latest(vg_name) AS vg_name latest(lv_name) AS lv_name latest(pve_storage) AS pve_storage latest(node) AS node latest(data_percent) AS data_percent latest(meta_lvs) AS metadata_percent latest(pool_size_bytes) AS pool_size_bytes latest(eta_to_full_hours) AS eta_to_full_hours latest(pool_state) AS pool_state BY cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pve_storage
    [ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:vm:disk" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
      | eval pve_storage=toString(coalesce(storage, store, pve_storage, ""))
      | eval mdx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(maxdisk, maxDisk, "0")), 10)
      | stats sum(mdx) AS sum_provisioned_bytes BY cluster_name, pve_storage
    ]
| join type=left max=0 vg_name, lv_name
    [ | inputlookup proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv
      | eval vg_name=toString(vg_name)
      | eval lv_name=toString(lv_name)
      | eval data_critical_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(data_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
      | eval data_high_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(data_high_pct, "80")), 10)
      | eval meta_critical_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(meta_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
      | eval meta_high_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(meta_high_pct, "80")), 10)
      | eval oversub_ratio_critical=tonumber(toString(coalesce(oversub_ratio_critical, "5.0")), 10)
      | eval oversub_ratio_high=tonumber(toString(coalesce(oversub_ratio_high, "3.0")), 10)
    ]
| join type=left max=0 node
    [ | inputlookup proxmox_node_owner.csv
      | eval node=toString(node)
    ]
| eval sum_provisioned_bytes=if(isnull(sum_provisioned_bytes), 0, sum_provisioned_bytes)
| eval pool_size_bytes=if((isnotnull(pool_size_bytes)) AND (pool_size_bytes>0), pool_size_bytes, 1)
| eval allocated_provisioned_ratio=if((isnotnull(sum_provisioned_bytes)) AND (sum_provisioned_bytes>=0) AND (pool_size_bytes>0), round(sum_provisioned_bytes / pool_size_bytes, 4), 0.0)
| eval d_crit=if(isnull(data_critical_pct), 90, data_critical_pct)
| eval d_high=if(isnull(data_high_pct), 80, data_high_pct)
| eval m_crit=if(isnull(meta_critical_pct), 90, meta_critical_pct)
| eval m_high=if(isnull(meta_high_pct), 80, meta_high_pct)
| eval r_crit=if(isnull(oversub_ratio_critical), 5.0, oversub_ratio_critical)
| eval r_high=if(isnull(oversub_ratio_high), 3.0, oversub_ratio_high)
| eval c_data=if((isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent >= d_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_meta=if((isnotnull(metadata_percent)) AND (metadata_percent >= m_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_ratio=if((isnotnull(allocated_provisioned_ratio)) AND (allocated_provisioned_ratio >= r_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_ro=if((pool_state="ro"), 1, 0)
| eval h_data=if((isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent >= d_high) AND (data_percent < d_crit), 1, 0)
| eval h_meta=if((isnotnull(metadata_percent)) AND (metadata_percent >= m_high) AND (metadata_percent < m_crit), 1, 0)
| eval h_ratio=if((isnotnull(allocated_provisioned_ratio)) AND (allocated_provisioned_ratio >= r_high) AND (allocated_provisioned_ratio < r_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_eta=if((isnotnull(eta_to_full_hours)) AND (eta_to_full_hours>=0) AND (eta_to_full_hours < 168), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case((c_ro=1) OR (c_data=1) OR (c_meta=1) OR (c_ratio=1), "critical", (h_data=1) OR (h_meta=1) OR (h_ratio=1) OR (c_eta=1), "high", (isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent >= (d_high - 5.0)) OR (isnotnull(metadata_percent)) AND (metadata_percent >= (m_high - 5.0)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_full_hours)) AND (eta_to_full_hours < 720) AND (eta_to_full_hours>=0)), "medium", (isnotnull(data_percent)) OR (isnotnull(metadata_percent)), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval sev0=if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="low"), "medium", if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="medium"), "high", if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="high"), "critical", if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="ok"), "low", sev0))))
| eval sev0=if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="critical"), "critical", sev0)
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, change_freeze, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, change_freeze, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval sev1=if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="critical") AND (c_ro=0), "medium", if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="high") AND (c_ro=0), "low", sev0))
| eval severity_bucket=sev1
| table cluster_name, node, vg_name, lv_name, data_percent, metadata_percent, allocated_provisioned_ratio, eta_to_full_hours, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
description: Monitors Proxmox VE LVM-thin pools where guest disks share a real-time block set and a separate metadata region: the REST storage list and per-node storage status show Data% and space totals, a node-side `lvs` poller is required for `metadata_percent` (the read-only class failure that can land before the Data% headline looks bad), and `cluster resources` disk sizes are summed to expose allocated:provisioned oversubscription against the pool LV size. The SPL multisearches `proxmox:storage`, `proxmox:storage:status`, `proxmox:lvm:thinpool`, and `proxmox:vm:disk`, coalesces snake_case, camelCase, and `used_fraction` to a unified `data_percent`, joins HEC `maxdisk` sums to each `storage` id, enriches per-pool thresholds and ownership from `proxmox_lvmthin_owner` (vg_name+lv_name) and `proxmox_node_owner` (node), computes `eta_to_full_hours` with a 24h `streamstats` window, lifts severity one bucket when ETA is inside seven days, and applies a `change_freeze_until` demotion that does not clear a thin-pool in `pool_state=ro` or mask missing-metadata gaps that already hit the critical case ladder. This is not Ceph RBD `POOL_NEARFULL` placement geometry (UC-19.2.33), not the vSAN operations-reserve / rebuild-headroom control plane (UC-19.2.27), not Cinder `provisioned_capacity_gb` math (UC-19.2.35), and not oVirt `committed` vs engine watermarks (UC-19.2.36) — the failure surfaces here are the LVM pool Data%, the thin-pool metadata slab, pve-thin-provisioning ratio, and optional read-only state after metadata exhaustion on the same PVs that hold data.
value: Proxmox LVM-thin lets you hand out more virtual disk size than the backing extent pool because guests usually grow slowly, but the pool can still run out in two different ways: the Data% hits 100% and every VM on the pool blocks new writes, or the metadata% fills while Data% still looks comfortable and the pool flips to read-only or stalls writes. Watching only per-VM charts or a cluster-wide “average” misses both failure modes, and a single bursty template clone can make Data% move fast. Pairing per-pool `data_percent`, `metadata_percent`, a declared-size oversubscription ratio, growth-based ETA, per-row thresholds, and a freeze window gives virtualization and platform teams a shared early signal with time to add NVMe, extend the pool LV, grow metadata with `--poolmetadatasize`, or rebalance guests before a weekday outage on shared infrastructure.
implementation: Ship three HEC pollers: datacenter `GET /api2/json/storage` to `proxmox:storage`, a loop over `GET /api2/json/nodes/{node}/storage` status for `proxmox:storage:status`, `GET /api2/json/cluster/resources?type=vm` paged to `proxmox:vm:disk`, and a per-node `lvs --reportformat=json` to `proxmox:lvm:thinpool` with 60s cadence. Add `proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv` and `proxmox_node_owner.csv` in `lookups/`, stanzas in `default/transforms.conf`, and schedule the saved search every five to fifteen minutes. Route PagerDuty on `severity_bucket=critical`, ServiceNow on high, Slack on medium, and send `vg_name`, `lv_name`, `data_percent`, `metadata_percent`, and `eta_to_full_hours` in the ticket. Standard remediation: extend the thin data LV in the right VG, then add metadata space if the event shows `metadata_percent` as the lead metric; document the change in the same app as the runbook and ITSI, one entity per (cluster_name, vg_name, lv_name) pool.
visualization: Row one: two radial gauges on the same row per pool, `data_percent` and `metadata_percent`, with colour bands that reflect `data_high_pct` and `meta_high_pct` from the lookup. Row two: horizontal bar chart of `allocated_provisioned_ratio` with reference lines for `oversub_ratio_high` and `oversub_ratio_critical`. Row three: timechart of `data_percent` and `metadata_percent` for the last 48h with a second axis for `eta_to_full_hours` if present. Row four: a freeze banner (single value) driven by `freeze_active=1` from the lookup. Dashboard drilldowns: click a bar to the raw `proxmox:lvm:thinpool` event and the PVE `storage:status` document for the same `node` and `storage`.

references.title: LVM: lvs thin pool and metadata (Red Hat / shared concepts)
Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector
Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833) — nix `lvs` / script fallback

knownFalsePositives: A fast clone or `qm disk move` of several large images can make `data_percent` jump for a few poll cycles even though the longer-term free pool trend is still safe — track two consecutive 5m breaching samples before a page, or add a 10m time modifier on non-production pools. A VM that is powered off can still have its `maxdisk` in `proxmox:vm:disk`, which keeps the allocated:provisioned ratio higher than a naive operator expects until disks are actually removed from the storage — treat that as a real oversubscription signal, not a broken feed. A scheduled `lvextend` or `lvresize` during a documented `change_freeze_until` can move Data% in ways that the freeze demotion is meant to downshift; keep `c_ro=1` as a no-freeze path. A storage migration in flight can count multiple disk rows briefly and inflate the sum — compare two polls, not one snapshot. A snapshot or template storm can spike `metadata_percent` without a matching guest-visible write error yet — that is a leading indicator, not a false alarm. When discard is applied asynchronously after a large guest trim, `data_percent` can step down a few minutes later, which can look like a self-heal when the poller and UI are out of phase.
hardware: Proxmox VE 7.4+ and 8.x on x86-64 server nodes with enterprise NVMe or SAS / SATA SSDs backing the LVM physical volumes that contain the `pve-thin` style pool — metadata and data for the same thin pool are carved from the same device group, so a metadata run is often a disk-space problem on those PVs, not a separate spool. Typical platforms include Dell PowerEdge, HPE ProLiant, Lenovo ThinkSystem, Supermicro, and Fujitsu PRIMERGX lines where a dedicated SSD tier holds the `local-lvm` style thin pool, sometimes alongside a ZFS rpool on separate devices for the OS (ZFS ARC and LVM are different stacks — this UC is LVM-thin, not a ZFS space-reservation story). Network attached “local” LVM on iSCSI is still valid, but the metadata pressure story is the same: small metadata over-allocation is normal; exhaustion still maps to the same `metadata_percent` and can force read-only. Keep NVMe and SSD firmware on vendor matrices because silent latency spikes show up as guest I/O pain before a headline Data% line moves.
exclusions: Ceph RBD or file-backed Ceph pools that Proxmox exports as a separate `rbd` or `cephfs` `storage` type belong to the Ceph `POOL_NEARFULL` and OSD class story in UC-19.2.33, not this LVM-thin pool and `lvs` path. Proxmox `zfspool` and ZFS on Linux datasets use different reservation and `zfs list` style metrics. NFS, CephFS, and iSCSI backed “file” or “dir” `storage` do not use LVM `data_percent` / `metadata_percent` the same way. A single non-clustered lab node with password login only and no API token discipline is not a good fit for the automation described here, even though the `lvs` poller can still be informative. CIM and Common Information Model data models are intentionally not part of the alert pipeline for this HEC design.
evidence: A saved report or alert such as `uc_19_2_34_pve_lvm_thin` on `index=virtualization` (or the agreed `proxmox` index) with 13+ months of online retention, the two owner CSVs in the same app as the saved object, a stored sample of `lvs` JSON and a matching `pvesm status` or UI screenshot for a sampled `vg_name` / `lv_name` row, and the alert action history in ServiceNow, PagerDuty, or a restricted `audit_evidence` index. Each firing should attach `data_percent`, `metadata_percent`, `allocated_provisioned_ratio`, `pool_state`, and `eta_to_full_hours` as named fields in the export.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — one entity per (cluster, vg, lv) LVM-thin pool with KPIs on data_percent, metadata_percent, ratio, and freeze flag

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab, raise `data_percent` above the row’s `data_critical_pct` in the `proxmox:lvm:thinpool` feed, or raise `metadata_percent` above `meta_critical_pct` with Data% still under the high water line, and confirm the next scheduled run returns `severity_bucket=critical` with the expected `vg_name` and `lv_name`. Add a second synthetic `proxmox:vm:disk` burst so `sum_provisioned_bytes` / `pool_size_bytes` passes `oversub_ratio_critical` and confirm critical even if Data% is not yet in breach.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Set `change_freeze_until` in `proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv` to a future ISO timestamp, reload, and confirm a row that was `critical` for Data% or ratio alone demotes to `medium` while a synthetically ingested `pool_state=ro` remains in `severity_bucket=critical` through the same window.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

Proxmox Virtual Environment (PVE) can present local and shared `storage` objects to the cluster. A `type=lvmthin` entry uses Linux LVM: a large logical volume holds the data region for the thin-pool, and a separate on-disk metadata area tracks which thin extents map to which guests. A common operational mistake is to watch only free terabytes in the Proxmox UI or a single guest graph; LVM-thin instead needs pool-level `data_percent` and `metadata_percent` from `lvs`, plus an oversubscription view that joins declared virtual size from `maxdisk` to the real pool. This scope is intentionally separate from the Ceph placement-group and OSD ratio story in UC-19.2.33, which is about a distributed RBD and CRUSH map, not the LVM2 thin-pool and its metadata slab. It is also separate from the vSAN `usable_used_pct` reserve story in UC-19.2.27, the Cinder over-subscription driver math in UC-19.2.35, and the oVirt engine `committed` field in UC-19.2.36, because the failure mechanisms are LVM2 thin device-mapper, not a vendor Ceph monitor class or a VMware admission slice.

PVE’s `/api2/json/storage` is a fast cluster-wide list; `/api2/json/nodes/{node}/storage/{storage}/status` is the node-local read that matches what operators compare to `pvesm status` on the shell. Neither endpoint alone replaces `lvs` for a portable `metadata_percent` in every PVE and `lvm2` build; always ship the `lvs` sidecar or accept a blind spot. Pollers should emit a stable `cluster_name` string (your org name for the PVE `datacenter` object), a `pve_storage` value that exactly matches the `storage` name in the API (for example `local-lvm`), a `node` for each per-node `storage:status` and `lvm:thinpool` line, the `vg_name` and `lv_name` of the `t` and `T` LVs that `lvs` reports, and a Boolean or string `pool_state` when the OS reports read-only, mapped in your HEC to `ro` for SPL.

Index recommendations: a primary `index=virtualization` or a dedicated `index=proxmox` for the API JSON, and `index=os` only for optional `nix` script captures of `lvs` and `pvesm` text when you are mid-rollout. Use one HEC token per index if your security team requires separation, but keep the sourcetypes below identical so the SPL does not fork.

The two customer-built CSV files must be present before production alerts. File `lookups/proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv` (transforms name `proxmox_lvmthin_owner`) columns, header row: `vg_name,lv_name,cluster_name,business_service,data_critical_pct,data_high_pct,meta_critical_pct,meta_high_pct,oversub_ratio_critical,oversub_ratio_high,owner_email,change_freeze_until`. File `lookups/proxmox_node_owner.csv` (transforms name `proxmox_node_owner`) columns: `node,cluster_name,business_service,owner_email`. The thin-pool file carries the tunable 90 / 80 / 5.0 / 3.0 class defaults; the node file is for human routing if only `node` is present in a sourcetype line.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

PVE has no first-party Splunk app that implements this full set of field-normalized HEC events; expect to install a private app (for example `TA_pve_lvm_thin` on a Heavy Forwarder) with your Python pollers, `default/props.conf`, `default/inputs.conf`, and `default/transforms.conf` entries for the two CSV files above.

Authentication: create a PVE user or token with at least the Auditor-style read scope for storage and resources. Pass `Authorization: PVEAPIToken=user@pve!tokenid=secret` to port 8006. Example `curl` against a manager node, with your org CA in `--cacert` for production, not plain `-k`:

```bash
curl -sS --cacert /etc/pki/tls/certs/pve-and-org-chain.pem \
  -H "Authorization: PVEAPIToken=splunk_reader@pve!pve_metrics=********" \
  "https://pve-mgr.example:8006/api2/json/storage"
```

and per node:

```bash
curl -sS --cacert /etc/pki/tls/certs/pve-and-org-chain.pem \
  -H "Authorization: PVEAPIToken=splunk_reader@pve!pve_metrics=********" \
  "https://pve-mgr.example:8006/api2/json/nodes/pve-05/storage/local-lvm/status"
```

`GET /api2/json/cluster/resources?type=vm` (paginate the `data` list until the `total` and returned rows are exhausted) is the `proxmox:vm:disk` source; flatten per-disk `maxdisk` and the backing `storage` name so Splunk can `stats sum(maxdisk) BY cluster_name, pve_storage`.

`proxmox:lvm:thinpool` is produced on each node, not in the PVE task manager, by running the literal command line:

```bash
lvs --reportformat=json -o lv_name,lv_size,data_percent,metadata_percent,pool_lv,vg_name --select 'lv_attr=~"V|t.*"' 2>/dev/null
```

A representative JSON line from the `report` array looks like: `lv_name: "pve--thin--0"`, `vg_name: "pve--ssd"`, `data_percent: "6.20"`, `metadata_percent: "1.00"`, `lv_size: "1.2t"` (your `lvm2` may emit strings with a unit; your poller should normalize to `lv_size_bytes` before HEC so Splunk can divide safely next to the API’s `total` field).

Python 3.10+ wrapper (stdlib only) that runs every 60s under systemd or a Splunk scripted input, posts one JSON HEC event per pool row with `sourcetype=proxmox:lvm:thinpool`, and sets the same `cluster_name` and `node` fields as the REST poller:

```python
import json, os, ssl, subprocess, time, sys, urllib.request, urllib.parse

def to_bytes(s):
    s = (s or "").strip().lower().replace(",", ".")
    if s.endswith("t"): return int(float(s[:-1]) * 1024**4)
    if s.endswith("g"): return int(float(s[:-1]) * 1024**3)
    if s.endswith("m"): return int(float(s[:-1]) * 1024**2)
    if s.isdigit():
        return int(s)
    return 0

def main() -> int:
    hec = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_URL", "https://idx:8088/services/collector/event")
    tok = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN", "")
    clu = os.environ.get("PVE_CLUSTER_NAME", "pve-prod-01")
    nod = os.environ.get("PVE_NODE_NAME", os.uname().nodename)
    cmd = ["lvs", "--reportformat=json", "-o", "lv_name,lv_size,data_percent,metadata_percent,pool_lv,vg_name", "--select", "lv_attr=~'V|t.*'"]
    raw = subprocess.check_output(cmd, text=True, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
    j = json.loads(raw)
    for rep in (j.get("report") or []):
        for row in rep.get("lv") or []:
            ev = dict(row)
            ev["cluster_name"] = clu
            ev["node"] = nod
            ev["pve_node"] = nod
            ev["data_percent"] = float(str(row.get("data_percent", "0")).replace(",", "."))
            ev["metadata_percent"] = float(str(row.get("metadata_percent", "0")).replace(",", "."))
            ev["lv_size_bytes"] = to_bytes(str(row.get("lv_size", "0")))
            ev["sourcetype_hint"] = "proxmox:lvm:thinpool"
            body = json.dumps({"index": os.environ.get("SPLUNK_INDEX", "proxmox"), "sourcetype": "proxmox:lvm:thinpool", "source": "pve:lvm:poll", "time": int(time.time()), "event": ev}).encode("utf-8")
            req = urllib.request.Request(hec, data=body, method="POST", headers={"Authorization": "Splunk " + tok, "Content-Type": "application/json"})
            ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
            urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=20).read()
    return 0
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

`inputs.conf` (Heavy Forwarder) for the REST poller (example) that hits `/storage` and the node loop, with `sourcetype=manual` in the `script://` and your code forcing `sourcetype=proxmox:storage` on each HEC line:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_pve_lvm_thin/bin/pve_storage_poll.py]
python.version = python3
interval = 300
index = proxmox
source = pve:storage:api
disabled = 0
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_pve_lvm_thin/bin/pve_lvm_node_poll.sh]
interval = 60
index = proxmox
sourcetype = proxmox:lvm:thinpool
disabled = 0
```

`props.conf` (Indexer or first parsing hop):

```ini
[proxmox:storage]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([
]+)
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TIME_PREFIX = "poll_utc":\s*"
TZ = UTC
[proxmox:storage:status]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TZ = UTC
[proxmox:lvm:thinpool]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TZ = UTC
[proxmox:vm:disk]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TZ = UTC
```

default `transforms.conf`:

```ini
[proxmox_lvmthin_owner]
filename = proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
[proxmox_node_owner]
filename = proxmox_node_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
```

Tertiary `Splunk_TA_nix` note: a script can echo the same `lvs` line to a monitored file, but the primary `metadata_percent` for alerting should be the `proxmox:lvm:thinpool` JSON above so the SPL coalesce is stable. Optional `lvs` text can land in a scratch sourcetype `proxmox:cli:thinpool` for Step 5 backfills only.

Step 2 — Create search and alert

Paste the `spl` field. Metadata% uses the same severity tier as Data%: both map to a fully blocked or read-only LVM2 thin-pool in real incidents because metadata exhaustion takes the map offline even when a headline Data% is still in the 60% band. The `inputlookup` joins embed default numerics in subsearches; if you already materialized `lookups` on the search head, you can replace them with `lookup proxmox_lvmthin_owner vg_name lv_name OUTPUT ...` in place — keep field names exactly as the SPL expects. `streamstats` window=288 at a five minute alert cadence approximates one day; when your actual poll is one minute, narrow `window` to 1440 for a true 24h span.

Alert schedule: 5m or 15m, `earliest` aligned with the `spl` (no `@m` / `@h` drift between alert window and the inner subsearches). Throttle: 60m on the tuple `(cluster_name, vg_name, lv_name, severity_bucket)` for non-`ro` events; do not throttle `pool_state=ro` below 15m until acknowledged. For Opsgenie or PagerDuty, include a deep link to the dashboard in the note.

`savedsearches.conf` snippet (names only, adapt your app id):

```
[uc_19_2_34_pve_lvm_thin]
action.email = 1
action.email.to = virt-oncall@example.com
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -35m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.fields = cluster_name,vg_name,lv_name,severity_bucket
alert.suppress.period = 60m
```

`alert` payload fields for ServiceNow: `u_vg=vg_name`, `u_lv=lv_name`, `u_data_pct=data_percent`, `u_meta_pct=metadata_percent`, `u_eta_h=eta_to_full_hours`.

The SPL body is repeated here for the runbook, identical to the `spl` key:

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.34 — Proxmox LVM-thin data%, metadata%, allocated:provisioned oversubscription. SOURCES: proxmox:storage, proxmox:storage:status, proxmox:lvm:thinpool, proxmox:vm:disk; LOOKUPS: proxmox_lvmthin_owner, proxmox_node_owner; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:storage" earliest=-30m@m ]
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:storage:status" earliest=-30m@m ]
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool" earliest=-30m@m ]
[ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:vm:disk" earliest=-1h@h ]
| eval is_vm=if(sourcetype="proxmox:vm:disk", 1, 0)
| eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
| eval node=toString(coalesce(node, pve_node, host, ""))
| eval vg_name=toString(coalesce(vg_name, vg, ""))
| eval lv_name=toString(coalesce(lv_name, thinpool_lv, pool_lv, lvm_tmeta, pool_lv_name, ""))
| eval pve_storage=toString(coalesce(storage, store, pve_storage, storageid, id, ""))
| where is_vm=0
| eval s_type=lower(toString(coalesce(type, st_type, store_type, "")))
| where (sourcetype!="proxmox:storage") OR (like(s_type, "lvm%thin%")) OR (s_type="lvmthin") OR (s_type="lvm_thin") OR (len(s_type)=0)
| eval ufx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used_fraction, usedFraction, "")), 10)
| eval data_api=if((sourcetype="proxmox:storage:status") OR (sourcetype="proxmox:storage"), if((isnotnull(ufx)) AND (ufx>0) AND (ufx<=1.0), round(ufx*100, 4), if((isnotnull(ufx)) AND (ufx>1.0), round(ufx, 4), null())), null())
| eval data_lvs=if(sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool", tonumber(toString(coalesce(data_percent, dataPercent, data_pct, "")), 10), null())
| eval meta_lvs=if(sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool", tonumber(toString(coalesce(metadata_percent, metadataPercent, meta_percent, metadatapercent, "")), 10), null())
| eval data_percent=coalesce(data_lvs, data_api, round(ufx*100, 4))
| eval tot_b=tonumber(toString(coalesce(total, total_bytes, tot_bytes, size_bytes, lv_size_bytes, "0")), 10)
| eval pool_size_bytes=if(sourcetype="proxmox:lvm:thinpool", tonumber(toString(coalesce(lv_size_bytes, lvSizeBytes, tot_b, "0")), 10), tot_b)
| eval ro_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(pool_state, poolState, status, st, "")))
| eval pool_state=if((like(ro_raw, "%ro%")) OR (like(ro_raw, "%read%only%")) OR (ro_raw="r/o") OR (ro_raw="ro"), "ro", if(len(ro_raw)>0, ro_raw, null()))
| sort 0 + cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name, _time
| streamstats window=288 current=t first(data_percent) AS d_24h_ago first(pool_size_bytes) AS ps_24h_ago BY cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name
| eval g24=if((isnotnull(d_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(data_percent)), data_percent - d_24h_ago, null())
| eval grow_pct_per_h=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24>0), g24/24, null())
| eval eta_to_full_hours=if((isnotnull(grow_pct_per_h)) AND (grow_pct_per_h>0) AND (isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent<100), round((100 - data_percent) / grow_pct_per_h, 2), null())
| eval eta_to_full_hours=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24<=0), null(), eta_to_full_hours)
| eval eta_to_full_hours=if((isnotnull(eta_to_full_hours)) AND (eta_to_full_hours<0), null(), eta_to_full_hours)
| stats max(_time) AS _time latest(vg_name) AS vg_name latest(lv_name) AS lv_name latest(pve_storage) AS pve_storage latest(node) AS node latest(data_percent) AS data_percent latest(meta_lvs) AS metadata_percent latest(pool_size_bytes) AS pool_size_bytes latest(eta_to_full_hours) AS eta_to_full_hours latest(pool_state) AS pool_state BY cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pve_storage
    [ search (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox OR index=os) sourcetype="proxmox:vm:disk" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
      | eval pve_storage=toString(coalesce(storage, store, pve_storage, ""))
      | eval mdx=tonumber(toString(coalesce(maxdisk, maxDisk, "0")), 10)
      | stats sum(mdx) AS sum_provisioned_bytes BY cluster_name, pve_storage
    ]
| join type=left max=0 vg_name, lv_name
    [ | inputlookup proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv
      | eval vg_name=toString(vg_name)
      | eval lv_name=toString(lv_name)
      | eval data_critical_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(data_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
      | eval data_high_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(data_high_pct, "80")), 10)
      | eval meta_critical_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(meta_critical_pct, "90")), 10)
      | eval meta_high_pct=tonumber(toString(coalesce(meta_high_pct, "80")), 10)
      | eval oversub_ratio_critical=tonumber(toString(coalesce(oversub_ratio_critical, "5.0")), 10)
      | eval oversub_ratio_high=tonumber(toString(coalesce(oversub_ratio_high, "3.0")), 10)
    ]
| join type=left max=0 node
    [ | inputlookup proxmox_node_owner.csv
      | eval node=toString(node)
    ]
| eval sum_provisioned_bytes=if(isnull(sum_provisioned_bytes), 0, sum_provisioned_bytes)
| eval pool_size_bytes=if((isnotnull(pool_size_bytes)) AND (pool_size_bytes>0), pool_size_bytes, 1)
| eval allocated_provisioned_ratio=if((isnotnull(sum_provisioned_bytes)) AND (sum_provisioned_bytes>=0) AND (pool_size_bytes>0), round(sum_provisioned_bytes / pool_size_bytes, 4), 0.0)
| eval d_crit=if(isnull(data_critical_pct), 90, data_critical_pct)
| eval d_high=if(isnull(data_high_pct), 80, data_high_pct)
| eval m_crit=if(isnull(meta_critical_pct), 90, meta_critical_pct)
| eval m_high=if(isnull(meta_high_pct), 80, meta_high_pct)
| eval r_crit=if(isnull(oversub_ratio_critical), 5.0, oversub_ratio_critical)
| eval r_high=if(isnull(oversub_ratio_high), 3.0, oversub_ratio_high)
| eval c_data=if((isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent >= d_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_meta=if((isnotnull(metadata_percent)) AND (metadata_percent >= m_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_ratio=if((isnotnull(allocated_provisioned_ratio)) AND (allocated_provisioned_ratio >= r_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_ro=if((pool_state="ro"), 1, 0)
| eval h_data=if((isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent >= d_high) AND (data_percent < d_crit), 1, 0)
| eval h_meta=if((isnotnull(metadata_percent)) AND (metadata_percent >= m_high) AND (metadata_percent < m_crit), 1, 0)
| eval h_ratio=if((isnotnull(allocated_provisioned_ratio)) AND (allocated_provisioned_ratio >= r_high) AND (allocated_provisioned_ratio < r_crit), 1, 0)
| eval c_eta=if((isnotnull(eta_to_full_hours)) AND (eta_to_full_hours>=0) AND (eta_to_full_hours < 168), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case((c_ro=1) OR (c_data=1) OR (c_meta=1) OR (c_ratio=1), "critical", (h_data=1) OR (h_meta=1) OR (h_ratio=1) OR (c_eta=1), "high", (isnotnull(data_percent)) AND (data_percent >= (d_high - 5.0)) OR (isnotnull(metadata_percent)) AND (metadata_percent >= (m_high - 5.0)) OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_full_hours)) AND (eta_to_full_hours < 720) AND (eta_to_full_hours>=0)), "medium", (isnotnull(data_percent)) OR (isnotnull(metadata_percent)), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval sev0=if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="low"), "medium", if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="medium"), "high", if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="high"), "critical", if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="ok"), "low", sev0))))
| eval sev0=if((c_eta=1) AND (sev0="critical"), "critical", sev0)
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, change_freeze, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, change_freeze, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval sev1=if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="critical") AND (c_ro=0), "medium", if((freeze_active=1) AND (sev0="high") AND (c_ro=0), "low", sev0))
| eval severity_bucket=sev1
| table cluster_name, node, vg_name, lv_name, data_percent, metadata_percent, allocated_provisioned_ratio, eta_to_full_hours, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

Step 3 — Validate

(1) On any cluster member with storage tools, `pvesm status` and compare the `lvmthin` or `PVE` pool `%` to `data_percent` for the same `storage` in Splunk after the next poll (expect sub-minute skew on 60s pollers, up to 5m on 300s storage lists). (2) On the same node, `lvs -o+lv_name,data_percent,metadata_percent,vg_name` and compare the `metadata_percent` to the `proxmox:lvm:thinpool` line with matching `vg_name` and `lv_name` — a mismatch of more than a rounding point means your JSON parser dropped a field. (3) Run a quick ad hoc `| tstats count WHERE (index=virtualization OR index=proxmox) sourcetype=proxmox:vm:disk BY pve_storage` to confirm the field exists and the sum is in the right order of magnitude versus `pool_size_bytes`. (4) `| inputlookup proxmox_lvmthin_owner | head 5` in Search should return rows, and a join to the pool table in Step 2 should not blank `business_service`. Expected “good” output shape: one row per `(cluster_name, node, pve_storage, vg_name, lv_name)` with `data_percent` in the 5–50 range in healthy dev pools, `metadata_percent` usually below 20 on fresh pools, `allocated_provisioned_ratio` between 0.5 and 3.0 in governed prod, and `eta_to_full_hours` null in steady state until growth is positive for a full 24h window. This validation path is LVM- and PVE-specific; it is not the Ceph `ceph osd df` walk from UC-19.2.33, not the oVirt storage-domain `available` / `used` / `committed` check from UC-19.2.36, and not the vSAN `usable_used_pct` UI cross-check from UC-19.2.27 — those are parallel sisters for other stacks.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Build a “PVE LVM-thin” dashboard tab beside the Ceph “RBD / OSD” tab so the operator can see which back end is the root cause when both panels exist. Panel set: (1) dual gauge row with `data_percent` and `metadata_percent` for the selected `pve_storage` token; (2) horizontal bar of `allocated_provisioned_ratio` for all pools, sorted by ratio; (3) `timechart` of `data_percent` and a second series for `metadata_percent` with a third for `eta_to_full_hours` on a right axis. Drilldown: token `pve_storage` to open a search with `sourcetype=proxmox:storage:status` and `sourcetype=proxmox:lvm:thinpool` in split tabs. Map ITSI entities to `cluster_name` + `vg_name` + `lv_name` with KPIs: pool Data%, pool Meta%, oversubscription ratio, freeze flag. On-call: route `severity_bucket=critical` to the virtualization SRE PagerDuty service with the ticket payload in Step 2. Escalation: link to a standard LVM2 extend runbook: `lvextend -L+SIZEG /dev/<vg>/<pool_lv>` and, for metadata, `lvextend --poolmetadatasize +SIZEG /dev/<vg>/<pool_lv>` (exact syntax should match your PVE and `lvm2` build — document the approved sequence for your org). If you also track change freezes in ITSM, mirror `change_freeze_until` there so a dashboard banner and the Splunk lookup stay aligned.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(a) `metadata_percent` is null for hours while Data% is present: the `proxmox:lvm:thinpool` poller died or the node script is not on every member that owns the `t` or `T` LVs. SSH to a suspect node, run the `lvs` line from Step 1, fix JSON to HEC, or clear a hung `lvm2` lock. (b) `data_percent` is always `0.0` while the UI is non-zero: the REST poller is using only `/api2/json/storage` without `/nodes/{node}/storage/{storage}/status` — the first path can lag or differ in `used_fraction` semantics; add the per-node `status` fetch for LVM-thin. (c) The oversubscription ratio is large even though the UI “looks” empty: stopped VMs and templates still have `maxdisk` on the pool — the ratio is a declared-size risk, not a used-space metric. Remove disks or change size to lower it. (d) A planned `lvextend` trips alerts: set `change_freeze_until` in `proxmox_lvmthin_owner.csv` to cover the window so `freeze_active=1` demotes non-read-only `critical` to `medium` and `high` to `low` per the SPL, while still allowing `pool_state=ro` to break glass. (e) A pool dropped to read-only mid-day: confirm `metadata_percent` in the 95–100 band at the time — LVM2 maps read-only for metadata, not the guest story alone; extend metadata per runbook, then re-check `lvs` after `pvesm` refresh. (f) HEC 403 / 4xx: token rotation, wrong index routing, or a Load Balancer stripping `Authorization` — `curl` the HEC with `Authorization: Splunk <token>` and a single-line JSON; fix LB rules or tokens. (g) Double-counted `maxdisk` after migration: two VM rows during move can overstate ratio for one poll — compare the previous poll, or add a 10m `dedup` in an experimental lab panel only. (h) PVE 7.x versus 8.x: field names in `cluster/resources` for disks may move between builds — extend the `coalesce` list in the `mdx` eval when the API object changes, then version-control a sample JSON per upgrade.
grandmaExplanation: Proxmox can promise more virtual disk room than the real pool has, a bit like overselling tickets. The shared pool can run out in two ways: the main space or the small map that remembers where every slice lives. We watch both, how “oversold” the pool is, and how fast the free margin is closing, so people can add disks or free space before writing stops for everyone on that pool.
